title: Roger’s Final Words Open the Pirate Era
description: On pages 3–5, this part follows the Romance Dawn flashback and focuses on how Luffy’s pirate dream is formed through Shanks, Higuma, and the straw hat promise.
scene: The manga opens by framing Roger’s execution as the spark that sends the world chasing the legendary treasure.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Gold Roger, the "king of the pirates," had achieved it all.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wealth, fame and power had all been his.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not surprisingly, the final words he spoke before they lopped off his head inspired adventurers throughout the world to sail the seas.

dialogue.speaker: Gol D. Roger
dialogue.text: MY TREASURE? WHY, IT'S RIGHT WHERE I LEFT IT...

dialogue.speaker: Gol D. Roger
dialogue.text: It's yours if you can find it... but you'll have to search the whole world!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...is about to witness a great era of PIRACY!

summary: Roger’s execution is used to explain why the world has entered an age of pirates. The chapter begins by tying Luffy’s personal dream to a much larger global legend.
